title: dysoxylum microcricum
intro: Dysoxylum microcricum, also known as the Burmese ironwood, is a deciduous tree species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in countries such as Myanmar, Thailand, and Malaysia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Dysoxylum microcricum include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and injuries. Dysoxylum microcricum is often prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
